input.headline: This painting is the most expensive work of modern art ever sold at auction

raw.headline: This painting is the most expensive work of modern art ever sold at auction

winner: I love this painting so much I’d sell my house for it—turns out, it’s actually *me* in the painting and I just bought myself a new home.

candidates.text: Why did the painting get a ticket instead of selling for millions? It was caught in a *framed* transaction!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'framed' are clever and specific to the headline, with a surprise twist that ties back to the context of art and auctions."}

candidates.text: I bought a $450 million painting today, but I don't think it's worth all the fuss—it just looks like a bunch of splattered puddles to me.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising comparison of expensive art to splattered puddles, concise and specific with a touch of wit."}

candidates.text: I love this painting so much I’d sell my house for it—turns out, it’s actually *me* in the painting and I just bought myself a new home.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with self-aware humor, concise and specific, ties back to the headline cleverly."}

candidates.text: Why did the million-dollar painting go broke? Because it had no frame of reference!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the play on 'frame of reference,' but it's a bit generic and doesn't fully tie into the headline about the painting being the most expensive. It lacks specificity and could be more surprising."}

candidates.text: It's $450 million, which is about what I'd expect if you gave Picasso a checkbook and a week in Paris with no rules.
